data.title: BlackRock Stock (BLK) Holds Steady Even as Redemption Pressure Jumps in Its Private Credit Funds
data.description:
BlackRock (BLK) moved to limit investor withdrawals from two of its private credit funds on June 12, 2026, after redemption requests exceeded their preset quarterly caps. On June 12, 2026, the asset manager activated liquidity gates on two major vehicles – the HPS Corporate Lending Fund (HLEND) and the BlackRock Private Credit Fund (BDEBT) – after investors asked to redeem more than 5% of shares for the quarter. Despite the pressure, BLK stock was up 1.3% on Friday.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

The pressure was sharp in BlackRock's HLEND fund, where investors sought to redeem 13.3% of shares, up from 9.3% earlier in 2026. BlackRock said it will repurchase only 5% of the fund, cashing out investors on a pro rata basis.

Further, the BDEBT fund experienced requests to withdraw 5.3% of shares. The fund stuck to its 5% cap, cashing out investors for about $83 million.

Why the Gates Came Down

Private credit funds lend directly to companies through long‑term, illiquid loans. Because these assets cannot be sold quickly without steep discounts, most funds cap withdrawals at 5% per quarter to avoid forced selling. When investors ask for more than that, managers put "gates" in place to protect the fund and keep the portfolio stable.

The jump in withdrawal requests is a clear sign that the once-booming private credit market is under pressure. Investors have grown more careful as concerns rise about loan losses, fraud cases, and exposure to software companies that may be at risk as AI reshapes the industry.

Is BlackRock a Good Stock to Buy Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on BlackRock stock based on nine Buys and two Holds assigned in the past three months. Further, the average BLK price target of $1,234.82 per share implies 20.21% upside potential.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: OpenAI's ChatGPT Makes History as Fastest App to Hit 1 Billion Monthly Users
data.description:
OpenAI, the artificial intelligence (AI) firm behind ChatGPT, has reached 1 billion monthly app users. According to estimates from Sensor Tower, a market intelligence firm, this milestone makes ChatGPT the fastest app ever to reach that level. It places the AI chatbot at a record-breaking level, surpassing the previous records held by Google Maps. The achievement highlights OpenAI's rapid global adoption, even as rivalry from other AI platforms grows.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

High conviction NVDA bears now have this Tradr ETF

ChatGPT Sets New User Record as AI Adoption Accelerates

ChatGPT reached 1 billion monthly app users in May, about 3.5 years after its November 2022 launch. This puts it ahead of all other apps in adoption speed. It also beat Google Maps, which took about five years to reach the same level.

In February 2026, OpenAI said ChatGPT had more than 900 million weekly active users across web and mobile platforms. The company also claimed it receives more than six times as many monthly visits and sessions as the next-largest AI platform. Sensor Tower ranked AI competitors behind ChatGPT, including Google's (GOOGL) Gemini, ByteDance's Doubao and Dola AI, and Anthropic's Claude chatbot.

Meta AI, a chatbot built by Meta Platforms (META), Claude, and ChatGPT, all showed strong user growth in the same month. Abe Yousef, a senior insight analyst at Sensor Tower, said model upgrades and shifting user demands are supporting wider usage of AI tools.

AI Usage Continues to Rise Despite Growing Concerns

OpenAI's partnership with the Pentagon has continued to raise public concern, with ChatGPT uninstalls surging by 295% on February 28, 2026. Around the same time, Claude briefly surpassed ChatGPT in U.S. downloads, hitting number one on the App Store as users turned to it in place of the OpenAI chatbot.

Meanwhile, there is also growing fear about the rapid development of AI models, even as demand for their use surges. Anthropic recently warned that AI models have surpassed human engineers in terms of coding. As a result, the firm urged regulators and companies to enforce stronger oversight. Even Pope Leo XIV has raised concerns over AI's impact on the environment and human jobs. 

What Is the Best AI Stock to Buy?

Some of the best AI stocks recommended by analysts include Nvidia (NVDA), Microsoft (MSFT), Google (GOOGL), and Meta Platforms (META). According to TipRanks analyst consensus data, all of these stocks are rated Strong Buys. This is because industry experts view these companies as leading players in AI growth and advanced technology. For more details on their price targets, performance metrics, and ratings, visit the TipRanks Stocks Comparison Center.
data.author: Joy Iyke

data.title: This Convenience Store Operator Just Raised Its Dividend 14%
data.description:
Casey's General Stores (CASY) has increased its quarterly dividend by 14%.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

The U.S. convenience store operator declared a distribution of $0.65 per share, up substantially from its previous dividend of $0.57 a share. The new dividend is payable on Aug. 14 to stockholders of record as of Aug. 1.

Casey's began as a general store in Iowa in 1968 and is today the third-largest convenience store operator in the U.S., with more than 2,900 locations, primarily in the Midwest. The company has raised its quarterly dividend for 27 consecutive years. That makes it a Dividend Aristocrat, a title reserved for company's that have increased their dividend for 25 straight years or longer.

CASY stock has a dividend yield of 0.29%.

Casey's Latest Earnings

The dividend increase was announced along with fiscal fourth-quarter earnings per share (EPS) of $4.37, which was well above the $3.31 a share forecast among analysts. Revenue of $4.57 billion beat the Wall Street consensus of $4.33 billion. Sales in the quarter were up 14% year-over-year.

In terms of guidance, Casey's said that it expects fiscal 2027 same-store sales to increase 2% to 5% with an inside margin above 42%. The company also forecast earnings growth of 8% to 10%. Wall Street had same-store sales growth of 3.9% penciled in for the company. CASY stock has risen 81% in the last 12 months.

Casey's Dividend Growth

Below is a chart that shows the growth of Casey's dividend in recent years. As one can see, the distribution has been growing steadily as the company prioritizes returning value to shareholders.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Why Is the Stock Market Up Today, 6/12/26?
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 (SPX) and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) are trading higher on Friday following a bout of early-morning volatility.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

The SpaceX (SPCX) initial public offering (IPO) is top of mind for investors. The IPO raised $75 billion at a $1.77 trillion valuation and was priced to open at $135 per share. However, SPCX is expected to open at around $170 on the Nasdaq (NDAQ) exchange due to strong demand. This could lift market sentiment and boost risk appetite among investors.

Consumer Sentiment Uptick Boosted by Lower Inflation Expectations

Elsewhere, the University of Michigan's June preliminary Index of Consumer Sentiment staged a strong comeback. The index came in at 48.9, above the estimate of 46 and rising from an all-time low of 44.8 in May.

"This measured improvement in sentiment was widespread, seen across age, education, and political party," said Surveys of Consumers Director Joanne Hsu.

In addition, expectations of inflation declined notably. The short-term expectation dropped to 4.6% from 4.8%. The long-term expectation tumbled to 3.4% from 3.9%.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Canadian Retailer Roots (ROOT) Is Exploring 'Strategic Alternatives' as Losses Pile Up
data.description:
Canadian clothing retailer Roots Corp. (TSE:$ROOT) says it is exploring "strategic alternatives" after reporting a first-quarter loss of C$10.1 million (US$7.23 million).

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

The Toronto-based company, known for its sweat pants and logo that depicts a beaver, had previously said that it is open to a possible sale of the company. However, no buyers have stepped forward to acquire Roots, whose financial situation is deteriorating.

For this year's first quarter, Roots reported a loss of C$0.26 per share compared with a loss of C$0.20 a year earlier. Sales in the quarter totaled C$42.6 million, up from $40 million a year earlier. The company has increased its marketing and offered discounts to attract consumers.

Bankruptcy Rumors

Management at Roots said the increase in revenue resulted largely from direct-to-consumer sales that totaled $35.8 million, up from $34.6 million a year earlier, while partner sales amounted to $6.8 million, up from $5.4 million a year ago.

In March of this year, Roots launched a strategic review of its operations in an effort to maximize value for shareholders. Now, the company says it is evaluating a range of alternatives for the business. Rumors and speculation online say that could mean a bankruptcy filing by Roots. The company reported a net loss of C$33.4 million in 2025. ROOT stock has declined 81% in the past decade.

Is ROOT Stock a Buy?

Not enough analysts cover Roots' stock, so instead we'll look at the year-to-date performance of its share price. As one can see in the chart below, ROOT stock has risen 49% this year after management said the company is for sale.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: RKLB, SPCE, LUNR: Space Stocks Fall Hard after SpaceX Blockbuster IPO Opens on Nasdaq
data.description:
Space stocks sank sharply on Friday as Space Exploration Technologies (SPCX), or SpaceX, made its long‑awaited public debut on the Nasdaq (NDAQ). Shares of Rocket Lab (RKLB), Virgin Galactic (SPCE), Intuitive Machines (LUNR), Firefly Aerospace (FLY), and EchoStar (SATS) all fell more than 10% at the open, giving back much of yesterday's big rally. Elon Musk and SpaceX President and COO Gwynne Shotwell rang the opening bell on Friday, marking one of the most anticipated IPO moments in years.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

Explore FLYT for 2X leverage on FLY

Hard Reversal After Yesterday's Surge

The sector had soared on Thursday as traders piled into anything tied to space ahead of the historic listing. But once SpaceX began trading, the sentiment shifted. Investors rushed to take profits, unwind speculative bets, and shift capital toward the SpaceX IPO itself.

Fund managers and retail traders are also selling other space names to free up cash for the massive offering. Many of these stocks had run up sharply over the past week, making them prime targets for "sell‑the‑news" moves now that the IPO is live.

Details of the SpaceX IPO

SpaceX's debut ranks among the biggest in market history, with the company raising $75 billion by selling 555.6 million shares at a fixed price of $135 each, giving it a valuation near $1.8 trillion. Investor demand was massive, with more than $250 billion in orders placed for the offering.

That means the deal was oversubscribed nearly four times, drawing intense interest from both big institutions and retail buyers. The size of the offering has led investors to reshuffle portfolios, adding pressure to other space stocks.

It is worth noting that trading does not start the moment the market opens. After the 9:30 a.m. bell, the stock went into an auction phase so buy and sell orders could line up and set a fair opening price. For a big IPO like this, steady trading can take a few hours to really start.

Is SPCX Stock a Good Buy?

Based on the rating of a single analyst covering SpaceX, Timothy Horan from Oppenheimer, the stock is a Moderate Buy. His price target of $190 implies about 41% upside from the IPO price of $135 per share.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Oil Exports from Middle East to Normalize by Late August, Says Goldman Sachs (GS)
data.description:
Wall Street investment bank Goldman Sachs (GS) says that oil exports from the Middle East are likely to normalize by late August now that it appears the Strait of Hormuz will reopen to commercial ships.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

Commodities analysts at Goldman Sachs also maintained their year-end forecast for Brent crude oil's price at $90 per barrel. The new outlook from the bank comes a day after U.S. President Donald Trump indicated that the U.S. and Iran have reached a peace deal that will see the Strait of Hormuz reopened within 30 days.

Goldman Sachs forecasts a five to six million barrels per day oil deficit in the current second quarter, but sees that deficit easing as commercial traffic resumes in the Strait of Hormuz, which is where about 20% of the world's crude oil is typically shipped.

Crude Oil Prices Fall

Crude oil prices have declined about 5% in the past 24 hours and dropped below $90 U.S. a barrel on reports that the U.S. and Iran have reached a deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. U.S. crude oil prices declined as much as 5% to $83.80 a barrel. Brent crude oil, the international benchmark, also fell 5% to $86.45 per barrel.

The peace deal reportedly includes a commitment from Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz within 30 days while the U.S. would lift oil sanctions on the country. Additionally, all U.S. military forces would retreat from Iran, and the U.S. and its allies will facilitate reconstruction of Iran at a cost of $300 billion.

Comparing Oil Stocks

Below is a chart comparing the stocks of oil majors Chevron (CVX), Shell (SHEL), and Occidental Petroleum (OXY). As one can see, all three stocks carry Buy ratings, although Chevron is the only one with a Strong Buy recommendation. Analysts continue to see upside potential in all three oil stocks.
data.author: Joel Baglole
